steps.title: Interpret the exponent
steps.content: $x$ is years; $3x$ counts how many compounding intervals there are per year — namely $3$ per year, so each interval is $\dfrac{1}{3}$ year $= 4$ months.

steps.title: Interpret the base
steps.content:
Each interval, the balance is multiplied by $1.005$ — a $0.5\%$ increase from the previous interval's balance.

So at the end of every 4-month interval, the balance grows by about $0.5\%$ of the balance at the start of that interval. That matches the correct answer.
